parameters.content:
## Zoom Attendance Tracker — Google Sheets + ClickUp + Telegram

For team leads, trainers, and operations managers who run recurring Zoom meetings and want automatic attendance logging with late-participant follow-up. When a Zoom meeting ends, this workflow fires automatically. Every participant is classified as Attended or Late, and each row is logged to Google Sheets. The host gets one AI-written Telegram summary per meeting. For every participant who joined late, a ClickUp follow-up task is created automatically so nothing slips through.

## How it works
- **1. Webhook — Zoom Meeting Ended** receives the meeting.ended event from Zoom
- **2. Set — Config Values** stores Telegram chat ID, Sheet ID, host name, late threshold, and ClickUp list ID
- **3. Code — Extract and Classify Participants** parses all participants and classifies each as Attended or Late based on join delay
- **4. Google Sheets — Log Participant Row** logs every participant as one row — runs for all items
- **5. IF — First Row Check** gates the Telegram path — only the first participant item passes
- **6. AI Agent — Write Telegram Summary** writes a concise meeting summary for the host
- **12. Telegram — Send Meeting Summary** delivers the summary once per meeting
- **8. IF — Is Participant Late?** checks each non-first participant for late status
- **9. ClickUp — Create Late Participant Task** creates a follow-up task for each late joiner
- **10. Set — No Action Needed** provides a clean exit for on-time participants

## Set up steps
1. In **2. Set — Config Values** — replace all six values: Telegram chat ID, Google Sheet ID, sheet tab name, host name, late threshold minutes, and ClickUp list ID
2. In **1. Webhook — Zoom Meeting Ended** — copy the webhook URL and add it in Zoom Marketplace under your Webhook Only App for the meeting.ended event
3. In **4. Google Sheets — Log Participant Row** — connect your Google Sheets OAuth2 credential
4. In **7. OpenAI — GPT-4o-mini Model** — connect your OpenAI credential
5. In **9. ClickUp — Create Late Participant Task** — connect your ClickUp API credential
6. In **12. Telegram — Send Meeting Summary** — connect your Telegram Bot API credential
7. Activate the workflow before running your next Zoom meeting

## Webhook and Config
Zoom sends a POST request here when any meeting ends. Config stores Telegram chat ID, Sheet ID, host name, late threshold in minutes, and ClickUp list ID — all set once here.

## Participant Extraction
Parses all participants from the Zoom payload. Classifies each as Attended or Late based on join delay vs the threshold. Outputs one item per participant — every downstream node runs once per person.

## Sheet Logging
Logs every participant as one row — runs for all items regardless of status. No condition. 14-column record per person.

## First Row Gate
Passes only the first participant item (isFirstRow = true) to the Telegram path. Prevents the host from receiving one message per participant.

## Telegram Summary
GPT-4o-mini writes a concise host-facing meeting summary covering topic, attendance counts, one observation, and one action tip. Fires exactly once per meeting via Telegram.

## Late Participant Actions
For each non-first participant, checks if status is Late. Late joiners trigger a ClickUp follow-up task with full meeting context and a next-day due date. On-time participants exit cleanly.

parameters.text:
=You are an assistant that writes short, professional meeting attendance summaries for a team host.

MEETING DETAILS:
Topic: {{ $json.topic }}
Meeting ID: {{ $json.meetingId }}
Host: {{ $json.hostName }}
Date: {{ $json.meetingDate }}
Duration: {{ $json.meetingDurationMin }} minutes
Total Participants Who Joined: {{ $json.totalParticipants }}
Joined On Time: {{ $json.attendedCount }}
Joined Late (after {{ $json.lateThresholdMinutes }} min): {{ $json.lateCount }}

Write a short Telegram notification message for the host. Follow this format exactly:

Line 1: Meeting just ended — state the topic name
Line 2: Blank line
Line 3: Date and duration
Line 4: Total participants count
Line 5: On-time count
Line 6: Late arrivals count
Line 7: Blank line
Line 8: One sentence observation — was attendance good or poor, anything notable
Line 9: One short action suggestion for the host based on attendance numbers
Line 10: Blank line
Line 11: End with: Full attendance log saved to Google Sheets.

RULES:
- Plain text only. No markdown. No asterisks.
- Maximum 80 words total.
- Be direct and professional.

assignments.name: result
assignments.value: =Participant {{ $json.participantName }} attended on time. No follow-up needed.

assignments.name: telegramMessage
assignments.value: ={{ $json.output || 'Attendance logged. Check Google Sheets for full report.' }}
